global.home_description: Brennan Shepherd — stylized environment work, Unreal shaders, and fantasy worlds built like a fashion lookbook.

recruiter-one-sheet.title: Recruiter One-Sheet | Brennan Shepherd
recruiter-one-sheet.meta_description: Quick read on Brennan Shepherd — stylized environment art, Unreal, and where to click next.

hero.headline: Environment art taste, technical art follow-through.
hero.lede: Stylized environments, Unreal material systems, and procedural dressing with clear production proof. Start with resume, then Sakura.

at_a_glance.headline: Where I fit.
at_a_glance.lede: Short on purpose. If you've only got a couple minutes, this is the gist.

cards.title: Stylized environment artist
cards.body: Mood, composition, routes you want to walk, materials that read from far away and still hold up in a close-up.

cards.title: Technical art curious
cards.body: Shader graphs, PCG, Geometry Nodes — I'm not pretending to be a senior TA, but I like building systems that save future-me from repainting the same rock.

cards.title: Fashion-fantasy editorial
cards.body: Think lookbook meets game world: gold trim energy, soft bloom, a little impossible geometry — but still readable.

best_proof.headline: Links I'd actually send.
best_proof.lede: Four clicks if time is short.

renders.lede: Pulled from whatever I last exported out of Unreal — updates when I run the ingest script.

portals.headline: Pick a door.
portals.lede: Freelance work, the Sakura write-up, or the full application hub.

sakura-case-study.title: Sakura Case Study | Brennan Shepherd
sakura-case-study.meta_description: Sakura Dream — shrine path mood, materials, and the axis I used to lay the scene out.

hero.headline: A walk I want to take.
hero.lede: This is the mood pillar of the portfolio: torii, stone path, soft light through petals. I built it to feel calm first — the technical notes are linked below when you want them.

intent.headline: Mood before metrics.
intent.lede: I tried to make the first ten seconds feel like arriving somewhere — not like opening a tech demo.
intent.feature_caption: Wide enough to breathe, tight enough that your eye finds the gate.

beats.title: Dusk, bloom, a little petal drift
beats.body: Enough glow to feel magical. Not so much that you lose the path.

beats.title: You can explain the route out loud
beats.body: Gate → path → corridor → platform. Not random props scattered for noise.

beats.title: One material family, lots of instances
beats.body: Lacquer, moss, stone, lantern warmth — same master graph, different MI tweaks.

axis.headline: The walk is the layout.
axis.lede: I use ZEN_SHRINE_AXIS as a spine so PCG and dressing know where not to clutter.

steps.label: 01 / Torii
steps.title: GB_ZEN_TORII
steps.body: The gate has to read from far away — that's the whole composition anchor.

steps.label: 02 / Sando
steps.title: GB_ZEN_SANDO
steps.body: Stepping stones and width — this is where traversal clarity lives.

steps.label: 03 / Kairo
steps.title: GB_ZEN_KAIRO
steps.body: Corridor rhythm, a pause before the last beat.

steps.label: 04 / Haiden
steps.title: GB_ZEN_HAIDEN
steps.body: The platform you were walking toward the whole time.

proof.headline: What's on the site right now.
proof.lede: Updates when I export from Unreal and run ingest — no hand-editing the grid.

craft.headline: What holds it up.
craft.lede: Click through if you care about shaders, PCG, or how this gets to the website.

cards.title: M_Master_Toon_Universal
cards.body: My main stylized surface — instances for moss, lacquer, petals, trim.

cards.title: Scatter with rules
cards.body: Heatmap keeps foliage off the path and pond so shots stay readable.

cards.title: Unreal → here
cards.body: Render script, ingest, these pages — so I'm not rebuilding HTML every capture.

next.headline: Keep going.
next.lede: Hub for the full application read, or the shader / PCG impact pages.

uses.headline: Not just portfolio filler.
uses.lede: Same frames end up on the homepage, Wix embeds, case studies, sometimes ArtStation — I try to shoot once, use everywhere.

hero.headline: I take on select freelance work.
hero.lede: Lookdev, hero frames, and breakdown pages. Digital asset packs are listed separately when they ship — start with scope, not a storefront.

packages.headline: Clear-ish offers.
packages.lede: Pricing is custom for now. These are the buckets I usually quote.

cards.title: Portfolio key art
cards.body: One polished environment frame + crops for web, ArtStation, social.

cards.title: Stylized material pass
cards.body: Direction, swatches, instance notes — fantasy surfaces, not PBR mud.

cards.title: Breakdown / case study
cards.body: Editorial web page for a tool, shader, or scene — same Melodia shell as my portfolio.

cards.title: Ornamental Kitbash
cards.body: 15 gothic & baroque UE5 ornaments in a preview pack. Purchase opens after store shots + Gumroad — store_live is still off.

steps.title: Brief
steps.body: References, mood, deadline, what file formats you need.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Scene, shader, or page — WIP check-ins if the job's bigger than a few days.

steps.title: Polish
steps.body: Composition, copy, responsive pass if it's web.

steps.title: Ship
steps.body: PNGs, UE assets, HTML — whatever we agreed on.

melodia-stage-character.title: Melusina Stage — Asset Passport | Brennan Shepherd
melodia-stage-character.meta_description: Melodia Portfolio Stage character plates with Nikki iridescence, Magical Girl ribbon chrome, design-system Asset Passport, and depth-tilt companion from the Blender EEVEE pipeline.

hero.kicker: Melodia Portfolio Stage · v7 · Nikki iri
hero.headline: Melusina — stage passport.
hero.lede: EEVEE glam beauty and grey wire topology first; the Asset Passport sits beside the plates as a short production card — triangles, meshes, shader lane — not a tech essay.
hero.eyebrow: Melodia Portfolio Stage · v7 · Nikki iri

outfit_board.label: Ability dress · character plate
outfit_board.title: Beauty first. Passport second.
outfit_board.body: Infinity Nikki photo-spot framing — beauty hero, grey wireframe, then the asset card.

beauty.title: Melusina beauty — EEVEE glam plate
beauty.caption: Pointer tilt · EEVEE glam · iridescent rim

hair_loop.label: Hair · AudVis 4s
hair_loop.title: EEVEE glam · 96 frames · 24fps · 4s
hair_loop.body: First 4 seconds of the AudVis hair sway — looped for stage.

hair_postcard.label: Hair FX · macro postcard
hair_postcard.title: Macro zoom · face / hair fill · 4s loop
hair_postcard.body: Tight social-crop zoom on the AudVis hair sway — face and fill detail, not a full-body plate.

zbrush_link.body: MatCap sculpt plates beside these glam keepers on the ZBrush breakdown.

melodia-melusina.title: Melodia Melusina — Game UI | Brennan Shepherd
melodia-melusina.meta_description: Melodia Melusina rhythm RPG UI — SheetMusicHUD continuous staff combat, Batch O scrollwork, notation grade flourishes, and Figma-synced battle chrome.

hero.headline: Celestial sheet music combat.
hero.lede: Turn-based rhythm RPG UI from the Grandmaster Figma library — SheetMusicHUD continuous staff + Batch O Baroque / SoftMG chrome, skill-ring + combo-burst accents (pearl/gold/iri-cyan on void), gothic portfolio frames, and motion prototypes for Unreal WBP_*. Batch N MusicalFiligree / NoteHead atlas slots are deprecated — do not re-bind. Atoms stay library-only; the beauty frame is a continuous score.

outfit_board.label: Outfit board · Game UI
outfit_board.title: Sheet music combat. Portfolio chrome.
outfit_board.body: Same Melodia lookbook language as Stage and Hub — the beauty frame is a continuous score framed in scrollwork before Unreal binds every WBP.

final_polish.headline: Game UI polish board.
final_polish.lede: Section-by-section gate before UE L3. Motion + baroque filigree expanded in Figma first; check each strip here, then bind.

steps.title: SheetMusicHUD
steps.body: Depth stack ≥4 · one hero zone lit · scroll rails no rotate break · iri grade ramp.

steps.title: Motion board
steps.body: IdleBreathe · PerfectStreak · BreakReveal · ULTReady · SoftMG_Baroque keyframed (2.4s).

steps.title: Baroque O
steps.body: CornerBaroque · DividerScroll · CrestBaroque · MedallionRosette · BraceVolute — denser than Batch O.

steps.title: iOS kit
steps.body: Portrait primary · ≥44px lanes · landscape nudge · thumb chrome clear of fingers.

steps.title: Batch O web
steps.body: Baroque PNGs bound on SoftMG / SheetMusicHUD · chips stay library-only · beauty = continuous score.

steps.title: UE L3 next
steps.body: Reimport atlas · bitmap filigree bind · Mobile BindWidgets · Command→Results WBPs.

phases.headline: Command · perform · resolve · breathe.
phases.lede: MelodiaCore phases — dominant zone lit, others dimmed. Windows from melodia_rules.json.

panels.title: Battle command — choose a song skill

field_title.headline: Exploration chrome & atelier title.
field_title.lede: Game/FieldHUD and Game/Title echo SheetMusicHUD lightly (parchment wash + mini staff) — Melusina stays visible; battle chrome does not swallow the character fantasy.

field_hud.dialogue: The path opens when the shrine bell answers.

nikki_reactivity.headline: Rhythm reactivity on the web.
nikki_reactivity.lede: Mirrors Figma Game/RhythmReactivityBoard (keyframed 2.4s — IdleBreathe · PerfectStreak · BreakReveal · ULTReady · SoftMG_Baroque). Beat bus drives scroll rails, filigree breathe, hitline pulse, ink-meter shimmer, streak edge, and notation grade halos while you play the SheetMusicHUD strip above.

strips.title: Motion/SoftMG_Baroque
strips.clef_hint: ClefBreatheToggle — type G F C G on the rhythm strip to swap SoftMG ⇄ Combat breathe

nikki_reactivity.channel_matrix: Beat → FiligreeBreathe, HitlinePulse · Bass → StaffShimmer, SPMeterShimmer · Grade → GradePopLuxury, NoteTrailIri · Streak → StreakGlowEdge (3/5/8) · Break → BreakCrestReveal · High/ULT → ULTArcPulse · Phase → PhaseDim · Lane → MobileLanePress · Shrine → ShrineCrestReveal (combo 8, SoftMG, one-shot) · Clef → ClefBreatheToggle (type G F C G, SoftMG ⇄ Combat)

batch_o.headline: Scrollwork chrome kit.
batch_o.lede: One framing jewelry system from Game/FiligreeBatchO — corners, wave, crest, rails, grade halos, border rail + parchment. Denser baroque expansion: FiligreeBatchO_Baroque. Not inventory chips; not 3D FiligreeRing kitbash.

components.headline: Atoms stay off the beauty frame.
components.lede: Grade flourishes, enemy telegraph, and skill codex mapped via code_connect_map.json — library units for Code Connect, not the default SheetMusicHUD composition.

ios_battle.headline: Mobile gameplay kit.
ios_battle.lede: Playable thumb-zone rhythm with SheetMusicHUD staff strip — safe areas, ≥44pt lanes, vertical highway. Open with ?mode=ios for full-viewport play.

envsandbox_assets.headline: Alphas & textures for Unreal.
envsandbox_assets.lede: Procedural exports from Figma-synced tokens → /Game/EnvSandbox/Alphas_Melodia + /Game/EnvSandbox/Textures/Melodia/GameUI. Run export_melodia_game_ui_assets.py then import_melodia_game_ui_textures.py in-editor.

pcg-system-impact.title: PCG System Impact | Brennan Shepherd
pcg-system-impact.meta_description: In-scene proof that PCG systems affect composition and traversal: route proof, heatmap, and capture slots that update from intake.

hero.headline: Prove the rules shape the route.
hero.lede: This page pairs the PCG heatmap with route-proof capture slots so reviewers can see the procedural system protecting traversal and composition.

impact_plates.headline: Route proof + beauty.
impact_plates.lede: These cards are fed by the Unreal intake JSON. Once the PCG route-proof plate is captured and ingested, it will appear here automatically.

next_capture.headline: Minimum proof for “PCG system” credibility.
next_capture.lede: These filenames match the capture brief. The goal: one plate that reads “procedural logic” and one that reads “final beauty.”

cards.title: ZEN_SHRINE_AXIS route proof plate
cards.body: Filename: pcg_zen_shrine_axis_route_proof_1920x1080.png — debug overlay or graph + a matching in-world crop that demonstrates the dressing rules.

cards.title: Exclusion heatmap
cards.body: Shows route-safe exclusion zones and the “why” behind your dressing density.

material-loop-gallery.title: Material Loop Gallery | Brennan Shepherd
material-loop-gallery.meta_description: Animated material instance loops — studio PP, pillar backdrops, profile-based swatch staging on M_Master_Toon_Universal.

hero.headline: Loops that show iridescence moving.
hero.lede: L_MaterialPreview_Studio captures — frozen NikkiDream PP, pillar cyclorama backdrops, and profile-routed swatch meshes. The pipeline runs in batches of 3 per night; run Tools\publish_material_loops.ps1 and walk away.

hero_validation.title: MI_ZenTrim_FlowersLots
hero_validation.lede: Sakura trimsheet with layer B flower density — the proof loop for roughness and iridescence reads.
